description: Add a --save-image <path> flag to inspect_layout.js that renders the layout tree as a wireframe PNG. The wireframe should be a scaled-down visual diagram (not a browser screenshot) representing the layout structure as colored rectangles on a canvas. The image dimensions should be proportional to the original viewport but smaller — scale to fit within 1200x900 px by default, preserving the aspect ratio of the captured viewport. Each node in the layout JSON tree (rect, background, border) should be drawn as a filled rectangle using its background color, with an optional border stroke if the node has a border property. Overlay the node's label text in a contrasting color. Use the 'canvas' npm package (node-canvas) installed into the skill's own node_modules at /workspaces/skills/layout-inspecting/scripts/node_modules/. Extend the existing self-install bootstrap (lines 38-83 of inspect_layout.js) to auto-install 'canvas'. Draw nodes in tree order (parents first, children on top) so child regions visually overlap parents. The output file path comes from --save-image. Supported formats: .png (always), .jpg if the path ends in .jpg/.jpeg. This flag is compatible with --output and --tui: all three can coexist (compute layout once, write JSON if --output is set, save image if --save-image is set, launch TUI if --tui is set).
acceptance_criteria: Running with --save-image out.png produces a PNG file
The image is a wireframe of colored rectangles, NOT a browser screenshot
Image fits within 1200x900 px by default while preserving the viewport aspect ratio
Each node is drawn as a filled rect using its background color, with border stroke if node has a border
Node labels are rendered as text in a contrasting color
The canvas library is auto-installed to the skill's own node_modules on first use
--save-image can be combined with --output and --tui without conflict
Outputs .png for .png paths and .jpg for .jpg/.jpeg paths

verification.value: Run: node /workspaces/skills/layout-inspecting/scripts/inspect_layout.js https://news.ycombinator.com --save-image /tmp/layout.png. Open /tmp/layout.png and verify: it shows colored rectangles representing layout regions (not a real screenshot), fits within 1200x900, has label text, and is visually recognizable as a wireframe.
